txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_Facts.fact: Movie critic Roger Ebert has noted in his online column that no matter where in Paris a film scene is set the Eiffel Tower will be visible in the background.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_Facts.fact: The plot of Night of the New Magicians from the Magic Tree House book series by Mary Pope Osborne had the protagonists searching the 1889 World's Fair beneath the newly constructed Eiffel Tower for Alexander Graham Bell, Louis Pasteur, Thomas Edison, and Gustave Eiffel, who had all gathered atop the tower.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_Facts.fact: In a commitment ceremony Erika Eiffel, an American woman, "married" the Eiffel Tower; her relationship with the tower has been the subject of extensive global publicity.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_Facts.fact: The Eiffel Tower ( EYE-fəl; French: tour Eiffel [tuʁ‿ɛfɛl] (listen)) is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It is named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the tower.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_Facts.fact: It opened in 1894 and is 158.1 m (518 ft) tall. Tokyo Tower in Japan, built as a communications tower in 1958, was also inspired by the Eiffel Tower.

txt_Facts.title: List of the 72 names on the Eiffel Tower
txt_Facts.fact: On the Eiffel Tower, 72 names of French scientists, engineers, and mathematicians are engraved in recognition of their contributions. Gustave Eiffel chose this "invocation of science" because of his concern over the protests against the tower.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_Facts.fact: In a Bill Nye the Science Guy episode titled "Magnetism", Bill Nye mentions two examples of "very tall metal towers" and the first one is the Eiffel Tower. The tower is shown onscreen when he mentions it.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_Facts.fact: In 2016, during Valentine's Day, the performance UN BATTEMENT  by French artist Milène Guermont unfolds among the Eiffel Tower, the Montparnasse Tower and the contemporary artwork PHARES installed on the Place de la Concorde.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_Facts.fact: Graffitists were also catered for: sheets of paper were mounted on the walls each day for visitors to record their impressions of the tower. Gustave Eiffel described some of the responses as vraiment curieuse ("truly curious").

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_Facts.fact: The tower is painted in three shades: lighter at the top, getting progressively darker towards the bottom to complement the Parisian sky. It was originally reddish brown; this changed in 1968 to a bronze colour known as "Eiffel Tower Brown".

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_Facts.fact: The film Category 7: The End of the World opens with the Eiffel Tower. The tower is ripped off its foundation and toppled by the tornado that hits Paris at the beginning of the film.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_Facts.fact: They later realize that Eiffel developed the tower to trap ghosts, making the Eiffel Tower the world's largest "ghost containment unit". By story's end, the ghosts were transmitted to the Ghostbuster's Primary Containment Unit back in New York, essentially de-haunting the Tower.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_Facts.fact: The Eiffel Tower has appeared frequently in works of fiction because of its iconic nature. Movie critic Roger Ebert has noted in his online column that no matter where in Paris a film scene is set the Eiffel Tower will be visible in the background.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_Facts.fact: The mayor of Blackpool, Sir John Bickerstaffe, was so impressed on seeing the Eiffel Tower at the 1889 exposition that he commissioned a similar tower to be built in his town.

txt_Facts.fact: 2011 In the 2011 Disney computer-animated film Cars 2, the Eiffel Tower appears "carified" and is different from the real life tower.

txt_Facts.fact: The tower is seen in Eurotrip, and mentioned when a protagonist asks whether they should "check out the huge line at the Eiffel Tower".

txt_Facts.fact: The Eiffel Tower ( EYE-fəl; French: tour Eiffel [tuʁ‿ɛfɛl] (listen)) is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It is named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the tower.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_Facts.fact: The tower is seen in Eurotrip, and mentioned when a protagonist asks whether they should "check out the huge line at the Eiffel Tower".

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_Facts.fact: In a Bill Nye the Science Guy episode titled "Magnetism", Bill Nye mentions two examples of "very tall metal towers" and the first one is the Eiffel Tower. The tower is shown onscreen when he mentions it.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_Facts.fact: The "oxidizer rocket" was originally planned by Krang to use to send the Technodrome to Earth, and when it failed, Krang thinks that it can be used by threatening to rust metal if the people don't surrender to Krang and Shredder. Even the Eiffel Tower gets blasted, and begins to collapse from rust.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_Facts.fact: Prior to the Eiffel Tower's construction, no structure had ever been constructed to a height of 300 m or even 200 m for the matter, and many people believed it was impossible.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_Facts.fact: 2011 In the 2011 Disney computer-animated film Cars 2, the Eiffel Tower appears "carified" and is different from the real life tower.

txt_Facts.title: List of the 72 names on the Eiffel Tower
txt_Facts.fact: On the Eiffel Tower, 72 names of French scientists, engineers, and mathematicians are engraved in recognition of their contributions. Gustave Eiffel chose this "invocation of science" because of his concern over the protests against the tower.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_Facts.fact: It opened in 1894 and is 158.1 m (518 ft) tall. Tokyo Tower in Japan, built as a communications tower in 1958, was also inspired by the Eiffel Tower.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower replicas and derivatives
txt_Facts.fact: The Eiffel Tower has also inspired other towers that are not close resembling replicas and therefore not listed here. An example of this is the Blackpool Tower on England's Irish Sea coast, one of the oldest towers inspired by Eiffel that opened in 1894, 5 years later, which proved that a tower could be a profitable tourist attraction.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_Facts.fact: The mayor of Blackpool, Sir John Bickerstaffe, was so impressed on seeing the Eiffel Tower at the 1889 exposition that he commissioned a similar tower to be built in his town.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_Facts.fact: They later realize that Eiffel developed the tower to trap ghosts, making the Eiffel Tower the world's largest "ghost containment unit". By story's end, the ghosts were transmitted to the Ghostbuster's Primary Containment Unit back in New York, essentially de-haunting the Tower.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_Facts.fact: Eiffel was to receive all income from the commercial exploitation of the tower during the exhibition and for the next 20 years.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_Facts.fact: In a commitment ceremony Erika Eiffel, an American woman, "married" the Eiffel Tower; her relationship with the tower has been the subject of extensive global publicity.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_Facts.fact: Prior to the Eiffel Tower's construction, no structure had ever been constructed to a height of 300 m or even 200 m for the matter, and many people believed it was impossible. These objections were an expression of a long-standing debate in France about the relationship between architecture and engineering.

txt_Facts.title: Gustave Eiffel
txt_Facts.fact: He is best known for the world-famous Eiffel Tower, built for the 1889 Universal Exposition in Paris, and his contribution to building the Statue of Liberty in New York. txt_Facts.title: European Union
txt_Facts.fact: Through successive enlargements, the European Union has grown from the six founding states (Belgium, France, West Germany, Italy, Luxembourg, and the Netherlands) to 27 members. Countries accede to the union by becoming party to the founding treaties, thereby subjecting themselves to the privileges and obligations of EU membership.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_Facts.fact: Due to the addition of a broadcasting aerial at the top of the tower in 1957, it is now taller than the Chrysler Building by 5.2 metres (17 ft). Excluding transmitters, the Eiffel Tower is the second tallest free-standing structure in France after the Millau Viaduct.

txt_Facts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_Facts.fact: The Eiffel Tower was the world's tallest structure when completed in 1889, a distinction it retained until 1929 when the Chrysler Building in New York City was topped out. The tower also lost its standing as the world's tallest tower to the Tokyo Tower in 1958 but retains its status as the tallest freestanding (non-guyed) structure in France.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: They later realize that Eiffel developed the tower to trap ghosts, making the Eiffel Tower the world's largest "ghost containment unit". By story's end, the ghosts were transmitted to the Ghostbuster's Primary Containment Unit back in New York, essentially de-haunting the Tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The "oxidizer rocket" was originally planned by Krang to use to send the Technodrome to Earth, and when it failed, Krang thinks that it can be used by threatening to rust metal if the people don't surrender to Krang and Shredder. Even the Eiffel Tower gets blasted, and begins to collapse from rust.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower ( EYE-fəl; French: tour Eiffel [tuʁ‿ɛfɛl] (listen)) is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It is named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is seen in Eurotrip, and mentioned when a protagonist asks whether they should "check out the huge line at the Eiffel Tower".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a commitment ceremony Erika Eiffel, an American woman, "married" the Eiffel Tower; her relationship with the tower has been the subject of extensive global publicity.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 2011 In the 2011 Disney computer-animated film Cars 2, the Eiffel Tower appears "carified" and is different from the real life tower.

txt_negFacts.title: List of the 72 names on the Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: On the Eiffel Tower, 72 names of French scientists, engineers, and mathematicians are engraved in recognition of their contributions. Gustave Eiffel chose this "invocation of science" because of his concern over the protests against the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a Bill Nye the Science Guy episode titled "Magnetism", Bill Nye mentions two examples of "very tall metal towers" and the first one is the Eiffel Tower. The tower is shown onscreen when he mentions it.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is painted in three shades: lighter at the top, getting progressively darker towards the bottom to complement the Parisian sky. It was originally reddish brown; this changed in 1968 to a bronze colour known as "Eiffel Tower Brown".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 2013 The Eiffel Tower is featured and parodied in the Kickstarter-funded low-budget animated feature film, Dick Figures: The Movie, based on and apart of the animated web series, Dick Figures. The very top of the tower is where the second piece of the ancient sword the main characters set out to retrieve is.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The children rush up the stairs of the Eiffel Tower to rescue the Mayor and the Mayor, after being rescued, presents the Eiffel Tower to the Parisians, who were excited. However, considering that the Eiffel Tower received criticism when it was first unveiled, the excited Parisians could have been a mistake made in the episode.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The children rush up the stairs of the Eiffel Tower to rescue the Mayor and the Mayor, after being rescued, presents the Eiffel Tower to the Parisians, who were excited.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The trio must take the Mayor of Paris to the Eiffel Tower in Olive's hot air balloon and when they get to the Tower, the Mayor floats up in the balloon.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: Even the Eiffel Tower gets blasted, and begins to collapse from rust. In the end, Donatello reverses Krang's device to turn the metals back to normal.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: When it was built, many were shocked by the tower's daring form. Eiffel was accused of trying to create something artistic with no regard to the principles of engineering.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower replicas and derivatives
txt_negFacts.fact: An example of this is the Blackpool Tower on England's Irish Sea coast, one of the oldest towers inspired by Eiffel that opened in 1894, 5 years later, which proved that a tower could be a profitable tourist attraction.

A: "The difference between the Eiffel Tower during the day and night is that it's lit up at night."

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The plot of Night of the New Magicians from the Magic Tree House book series by Mary Pope Osborne had the protagonists searching the 1889 World's Fair beneath the newly constructed Eiffel Tower for Alexander Graham Bell, Louis Pasteur, Thomas Edison, and Gustave Eiffel, who had all gathered atop the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The object of the transmissions was to measure the difference in longitude between Paris and Washington, D.C.. Today, radio and digital television signals are transmitted from the Eiffel Tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Kirk Kerkorian
txt_negFacts.fact: Kerkorian was one of the important figures in the shaping of Las Vegas and, with architect Martin Stern Jr. described as the "father of the mega-resort". He built the world's largest hotel in Las Vegas three times: the International Hotel (opened in 1969), the MGM Grand Hotel (1973) and the MGM Grand (1993).

txt_negFacts.title: Jean Joho
txt_negFacts.fact: Jean Joho is a French-American chef and restaurateur. He is chef/proprietor of Everest in Chicago (founded in 1986, closed 2020), Paris Club Bistro & Bar and Studio Paris in Chicago, The Eiffel Tower Restaurant in Las Vegas, and Brasserie JO in Boston.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In the 2010 computer-animated comedy film Despicable Me, the Eiffel Tower is shown being projected by the French Army and a mime after the Great Pyramid of Giza is stolen by Vector. Later on, Gru shows the Minions that they accomplished in stealing the tower, albeit the replica from Paris Las Vegas.

txt_negFacts.title: Jean Joho
txt_negFacts.fact: He is chef/proprietor of Everest in Chicago (founded in 1986, closed 2020), Paris Club Bistro & Bar and Studio Paris in Chicago, The Eiffel Tower Restaurant in Las Vegas, and Brasserie JO in Boston.

txt_negFacts.title: Paris Las Vegas
txt_negFacts.fact: In the 2007 film Resident Evil: Extinction, Paris is seen buried in sand, along with the rest of Las Vegas, several years after a zombie apocalypse. One of the characters climbs the Eiffel Tower replica to escape a horde of zombies.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is seen in Eurotrip, and mentioned when a protagonist asks whether they should "check out the huge line at the Eiffel Tower".

txt_negFacts.title: Paris Las Vegas
txt_negFacts.fact: Paris Las Vegas opened on September 1, 1999, with fireworks being shot from the Eiffel Tower. French actress Catherine Deneuve flipped a switch, turning on all of Paris' lights, including the various crystal chandeliers in the main lobby.

txt_negFacts.title: Grand Hotel (1932 film)
txt_negFacts.fact: In his memoir Adventures in the Screen Trade, screenwriter William Goldman recalls being contacted by director Norman Jewison about doing a remake of the film as a musical, to be set at the original MGM Grand Hotel in Las Vegas (now Bally's).

txt_negFacts.title: Bally's Las Vegas
txt_negFacts.fact: Bally's Las Vegas (formerly MGM Grand Hotel and Casino) is a casino hotel on the Las Vegas Strip in Paradise, Nevada. It is owned and operated by Caesars Entertainment.

txt_negFacts.title: Kirk Kerkorian
txt_negFacts.fact: He built the world's largest hotel in Las Vegas three times: the International Hotel (opened in 1969), the MGM Grand Hotel (1973) and the MGM Grand (1993).

txt_negFacts.title: MGM Resorts International
txt_negFacts.fact: When the Las Vegas Hilton, the casino he had built, subsequently became the most successful hotel in Las Vegas, Kerkorian was inspired to lead the studio into the gambling industry. It opened the original MGM Grand Hotel and Casino (now Bally's Las Vegas) in 1973.

txt_negFacts.title: MGM Grand fire
txt_negFacts.fact: A second tower opened in 1981. The MGM Grand was sold in 1986 to Bally Manufacturing, which changed the name to Bally's Las Vegas.

txt_negFacts.title: MGM Resorts International
txt_negFacts.fact: It opened the original MGM Grand Hotel and Casino (now Bally's Las Vegas) in 1973. The MGM Grand Reno followed in 1978.By 1979, the two hotel-casinos accounted for most of MGM's income, and the company announced a plan to split itself in two.

txt_negFacts.title: Grand Hotel (1932 film)
txt_negFacts.fact: A movie musical remake, to take place at Las Vegas' MGM Grand Hotel, directed by Norman Jewison, was considered in 1977, and again in 1981, but eventually fell through.

txt_negFacts.title: MGM Grand Las Vegas
txt_negFacts.fact: When the MGM Grand opened, the intention was to create a destination hotel in the Las Vegas area by including the MGM Grand Adventures Theme Park behind the casino.

txt_negFacts.title: Kirk Kerkorian
txt_negFacts.fact: He built the world's largest hotel in Las Vegas three times: the International Hotel (opened in 1969), the MGM Grand Hotel (1973) and the MGM Grand (1993). He purchased the Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer movie studio in 1979.

txt_negFacts.fact: 1987 An episode of The Real Ghostbusters entitled "The Ghostbusters in Paris" had the tower become haunted, causing a French government official to go to New York and enlist the help of the Ghostbusters. They later realize that Eiffel developed the tower to trap ghosts, making the Eiffel Tower the world's largest "ghost containment unit".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower ( EYE-fəl; French: tour Eiffel [tuʁ‿ɛfɛl] (listen)) is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It is named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 2011 In the 2011 Disney computer-animated film Cars 2, the Eiffel Tower appears "carified" and is different from the real life tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In Paris When It Sizzles, Richard's movie within the movie is called "The Girl Who Stole the Eiffel Tower" and several key scenes take place there.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In the 2010 computer-animated comedy film Despicable Me, the Eiffel Tower is shown being projected by the French Army and a mime after the Great Pyramid of Giza is stolen by Vector. Later on, Gru shows the Minions that they accomplished in stealing the tower, albeit the replica from Paris Las Vegas.

txt_negFacts.fact: “Paris” is now written in big white bold letters above a large Eiffel Tower, clearly putting forward the brand “Paris” instead of “Paris Saint-Germain.” Underneath it, “Saint-Germain” is written in smaller letters below the fleur de lys.

txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower ( EYE-fəl; French: tour Eiffel [tuʁ‿ɛfɛl] (listen)) is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Cedar Fair)
txt_negFacts.fact: Both of the replicas at Kings Dominion and Kings Island are one-third replicas of the Eiffel Tower in Paris. They were both constructed by then-owner Taft Broadcasting.

txt_negFacts.title: Gustave Eiffel
txt_negFacts.fact: He is best known for the world-famous Eiffel Tower, built for the 1889 Universal Exposition in Paris, and his contribution to building the Statue of Liberty in New York.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a commitment ceremony Erika Eiffel, an American woman, "married" the Eiffel Tower; her relationship with the tower has been the subject of extensive global publicity.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 1996 - In the 1996 PlayStation game Twisted Metal 2 Players are able blow up the Eiffel Tower using a Remote Bomb

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Paris, Texas)
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower was constructed in 1993. It is a scale model of the Eiffel Tower in Paris, France, and is less than one tenth of the height of the original.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The film Category 7: The End of the World opens with the Eiffel Tower. The tower is ripped off its foundation and toppled by the tornado that hits Paris at the beginning of the film.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Paris, Tennessee)
txt_negFacts.fact: Eiffel Tower is a landmark in the city of Paris, Tennessee. This structure is a  model of the Eiffel Tower in Paris, France.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower ( EYE-fəl; French: tour Eiffel [tuʁ‿ɛfɛl] (listen)) is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It is named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: They later realize that Eiffel developed the tower to trap ghosts, making the Eiffel Tower the world's largest "ghost containment unit". By story's end, the ghosts were transmitted to the Ghostbuster's Primary Containment Unit back in New York, essentially de-haunting the Tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: Graffitists were also catered for: sheets of paper were mounted on the walls each day for visitors to record their impressions of the tower. Gustave Eiffel described some of the responses as vraiment curieuse ("truly curious").

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In the 2010 computer-animated comedy film Despicable Me, the Eiffel Tower is shown being projected by the French Army and a mime after the Great Pyramid of Giza is stolen by Vector. Later on, Gru shows the Minions that they accomplished in stealing the tower, albeit the replica from Paris Las Vegas.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The plot of Night of the New Magicians from the Magic Tree House book series by Mary Pope Osborne had the protagonists searching the 1889 World's Fair beneath the newly constructed Eiffel Tower for Alexander Graham Bell, Louis Pasteur, Thomas Edison, and Gustave Eiffel, who had all gathered atop the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is seen in Eurotrip, and mentioned when a protagonist asks whether they should "check out the huge line at the Eiffel Tower".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a commitment ceremony Erika Eiffel, an American woman, "married" the Eiffel Tower; her relationship with the tower has been the subject of extensive global publicity.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 2011 In the 2011 Disney computer-animated film Cars 2, the Eiffel Tower appears "carified" and is different from the real life tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a Bill Nye the Science Guy episode titled "Magnetism", Bill Nye mentions two examples of "very tall metal towers" and the first one is the Eiffel Tower. The tower is shown onscreen when he mentions it.

txt_negFacts.title: List of the 72 names on the Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: On the Eiffel Tower, 72 names of French scientists, engineers, and mathematicians are engraved in recognition of their contributions. Gustave Eiffel chose this "invocation of science" because of his concern over the protests against the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is painted in three shades: lighter at the top, getting progressively darker towards the bottom to complement the Parisian sky. It was originally reddish brown; this changed in 1968 to a bronze colour known as "Eiffel Tower Brown".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 2013 The Eiffel Tower is featured and parodied in the Kickstarter-funded low-budget animated feature film, Dick Figures: The Movie, based on and apart of the animated web series, Dick Figures. The very top of the tower is where the second piece of the ancient sword the main characters set out to retrieve is.

txt_negFacts.title: Westgate Las Vegas
txt_negFacts.fact: Westgate CEO David Siegel was hoisted to the top of the hotel's sign the following day to begin removing the letters "LVH" to make way for the property's new name, Westgate Las Vegas.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Paris, Texas)
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower was constructed in 1993. It is a scale model of the Eiffel Tower in Paris, France, and is less than one tenth of the height of the original.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The film Category 7: The End of the World opens with the Eiffel Tower. The tower is ripped off its foundation and toppled by the tornado that hits Paris at the beginning of the film.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The mayor of Blackpool, Sir John Bickerstaffe, was so impressed on seeing the Eiffel Tower at the 1889 exposition that he commissioned a similar tower to be built in his town.

txt_negFacts.fact: The trio must take the Mayor of Paris to the Eiffel Tower in Olive's hot air balloon and when they get to the Tower, the Mayor floats up in the balloon.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: Eiffel had a permit for the tower to stand for 20 years. It was to be dismantled in 1909, when its ownership would revert to the City of Paris.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower ( EYE-fəl; French: tour Eiffel [tuʁ‿ɛfɛl] (listen)) is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It is named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is seen in Eurotrip, and mentioned when a protagonist asks whether they should "check out the huge line at the Eiffel Tower".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a commitment ceremony Erika Eiffel, an American woman, "married" the Eiffel Tower; her relationship with the tower has been the subject of extensive global publicity.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 2011 In the 2011 Disney computer-animated film Cars 2, the Eiffel Tower appears "carified" and is different from the real life tower.

txt_negFacts.title: List of the 72 names on the Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: On the Eiffel Tower, 72 names of French scientists, engineers, and mathematicians are engraved in recognition of their contributions. Gustave Eiffel chose this "invocation of science" because of his concern over the protests against the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is painted in three shades: lighter at the top, getting progressively darker towards the bottom to complement the Parisian sky. It was originally reddish brown; this changed in 1968 to a bronze colour known as "Eiffel Tower Brown".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a Bill Nye the Science Guy episode titled "Magnetism", Bill Nye mentions two examples of "very tall metal towers" and the first one is the Eiffel Tower. The tower is shown onscreen when he mentions it.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The plot of Night of the New Magicians from the Magic Tree House book series by Mary Pope Osborne had the protagonists searching the 1889 World's Fair beneath the newly constructed Eiffel Tower for Alexander Graham Bell, Louis Pasteur, Thomas Edison, and Gustave Eiffel, who had all gathered atop the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower and the Montparnasse Tower also light up to the rhythm of PHARES. This is the first time that the Eiffel Tower has interacted with a work of art.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: Gustave Eiffel responded to these criticisms by comparing his tower to the Egyptian pyramids: "My tower will be the tallest edifice ever erected by man.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The mayor of Blackpool, Sir John Bickerstaffe, was so impressed on seeing the Eiffel Tower at the 1889 exposition that he commissioned a similar tower to be built in his town.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: It opened in 1894 and is 158.1 m (518 ft) tall. Tokyo Tower in Japan, built as a communications tower in 1958, was also inspired by the Eiffel Tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Paris, Texas)
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower was constructed in 1993. It is a scale model of the Eiffel Tower in Paris, France, and is less than one tenth of the height of the original.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The film Category 7: The End of the World opens with the Eiffel Tower. The tower is ripped off its foundation and toppled by the tornado that hits Paris at the beginning of the film.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower ( EYE-fəl; French: tour Eiffel [tuʁ‿ɛfɛl] (listen)) is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The plot of Night of the New Magicians from the Magic Tree House book series by Mary Pope Osborne had the protagonists searching the 1889 World's Fair beneath the newly constructed Eiffel Tower for Alexander Graham Bell, Louis Pasteur, Thomas Edison, and Gustave Eiffel, who had all gathered atop the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Cedar Fair)
txt_negFacts.fact: Unlike Kings Dominion, Kings Island features Fast Lane which enables visitors to get to the front of the line without waiting. It is also a 1/3 scale of the real Eiffel Tower in Paris, France.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower and the Montparnasse Tower also light up to the rhythm of PHARES. This is the first time that the Eiffel Tower has interacted with a work of art.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: Gustave Eiffel responded to these criticisms by comparing his tower to the Egyptian pyramids: "My tower will be the tallest edifice ever erected by man.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The mayor of Blackpool, Sir John Bickerstaffe, was so impressed on seeing the Eiffel Tower at the 1889 exposition that he commissioned a similar tower to be built in his town.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: It opened in 1894 and is 158.1 m (518 ft) tall. Tokyo Tower in Japan, built as a communications tower in 1958, was also inspired by the Eiffel Tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Paris, Texas)
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower was constructed in 1993. It is a scale model of the Eiffel Tower in Paris, France, and is less than one tenth of the height of the original.

txt_negFacts.title: List of hotel fires in the United States
txt_negFacts.fact: On November 21, 1980, a fire broke out in the MGM Grand Hotel (now Bally's Las Vegas) in Paradise, Nevada, killing 85 people, most through smoke inhalation. It remains the worst disaster in Nevada history, and the third-worst hotel fire in modern U.S. history.

txt_negFacts.fact: Paris Las Vegas opened on September 1, 1999, with fireworks being shot from the Eiffel Tower. French actress Catherine Deneuve flipped a switch, turning on all of Paris' lights, including the various crystal chandeliers in the main lobby.

txt_negFacts.title: The Claridge Hotel (Atlantic City)
txt_negFacts.fact: Beginning in 1981, Claridge's operated for many years as a casino, known first as "Del Webb's Claridge Hotel and Casino", then as "Claridge Hotel and Casino". The hotel was acquired by Bally's on December 30, 2002, as a hotel tower of Bally's Atlantic City.

txt_negFacts.title: The Claridge Hotel (Atlantic City)
txt_negFacts.fact: As a result, the Claridge became a hotel tower for Bally's. In 2009, all of the Claridge's own restaurants ceased operation, but a small cafe, "The Corner Cafe", that continues to operate in the Gateway area that connects the Claridge Tower with Bally's.

txt_negFacts.title: Bally Manufacturing

txt_negFacts.title: Grand Sierra Resort
txt_negFacts.fact: In March, the hotel was placed on the market in an attempt to aid their debt by unloading the non-revenue producing Bally's Reno, sister hotel Bally's Las Vegas was far more superior in terms of revenues.

txt_negFacts.title: Bally Manufacturing
txt_negFacts.fact: The spin-off was completed in January 1996, with Bally Total Fitness becoming a separate company. In May 1995, Bally Entertainment announced plans to develop Paris Las Vegas, a new casino hotel next to Bally's Las Vegas.

txt_negFacts.title: Bally's Atlantic City
txt_negFacts.fact: In 1989, Bally constructed a 750-room hotel tower in a modern style, with an exterior of light pink glass. On July 2, 1997, The Wild Wild West Casino opened as the second casino at Bally's.

txt_negFacts.title: Resorts World Las Vegas
txt_negFacts.fact: The resort also contains the largest Conrad hotel in the world – Conrad Las Vegas at Resorts World – with 1,496 rooms. Another portion of the hotel building, with the remaining 236 rooms, is branded as the high-end Crockfords Las Vegas - part of LXR Hotel & Resorts.

txt_negFacts.title: Bally's Atlantic City
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower's 500 hotel rooms continued to be used for Bally's guests until it was sold in 2013 to be reopened as the independent Claridge Hotel.

txt_negFacts.title: Bally's Atlantic City
txt_negFacts.fact: However, in an effort to offset costs and open the casino as soon as possible, the Dennis Hotel was retained to serve as the temporary hotel for Bally's until a new tower could be built.

txt_negFacts.title: Leg shaving
txt_negFacts.fact: Leg shaving is the practice of removing leg hair by shaving the hair off using a razor or electric shaver.   In addition, some people remove leg hair using waxing, sugaring, depilatories, epilators or other depilation devices, or lasers, but shaving remains the least expensive and one of the least painful methods.

txt_negFacts.title: The Strat (Las Vegas)

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower was the world's tallest structure when completed in 1889, a distinction it retained until 1929 when the Chrysler Building in New York City was topped out. The tower also lost its standing as the world's tallest tower to the Tokyo Tower in 1958 but retains its status as the tallest freestanding (non-guyed) structure in France.

txt_negFacts.title: History of the world's tallest buildings
txt_negFacts.fact: Similarly, the Eiffel Tower was the world's tallest structure from 1889, when it was built, until 1930, when the Chrysler Building was built.

txt_negFacts.title: History of the world's tallest buildings
txt_negFacts.fact: For thousands of years, the Great Pyramid in Egypt was the tallest structure in the world, but the Great Pyramid is not considered a building since it is not habitable. Similarly, the Eiffel Tower was the world's tallest structure from 1889, when it was built, until 1930, when the Chrysler Building was built.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower was the world's tallest structure when completed in 1889, a distinction it retained until 1929 when the Chrysler Building in New York City was topped out.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: Due to the addition of a broadcasting aerial at the top of the tower in 1957, it is now taller than the Chrysler Building by 5.2 metres (17 ft). Excluding transmitters, the Eiffel Tower is the second tallest free-standing structure in France after the Millau Viaduct.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: During its construction, the Eiffel Tower surpassed the Washington Monument to become the tallest man-made structure in the world, a title it held for 41 years until the Chrysler Building in New York City was finished in 1930.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: A bust of Gustave Eiffel by Antoine Bourdelle was unveiled at the base of the north leg on 2 May 1929. In 1930, the tower lost the title of the world's tallest structure when the Chrysler Building in New York City was completed.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: Its base is square, measuring 125 metres (410 ft) on each side. During its construction, the Eiffel Tower surpassed the Washington Monument to become the tallest man-made structure in the world, a title it held for 41 years until the Chrysler Building in New York City was finished in 1930.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower

txt_negFacts.title: Paris Las Vegas

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Paris, Texas)
txt_negFacts.fact: This replica was built in 1993, the same year as another one in the United States, the Eiffel Tower in Paris, Tennessee. Original plans made the construction five feet taller than the one in Tennessee and a cowboy hat added to the top, in a competitive spirit, made the total height 65 feet.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Paris, Texas)

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: Due to the addition of a broadcasting aerial at the top of the tower in 1957, it is now taller than the Chrysler Building by 5.2 metres (17 ft). Excluding transmitters, the Eiffel Tower is the second tallest free-standing structure in France after the Millau Viaduct.

txt_negFacts.title: Paris Las Vegas
txt_negFacts.fact: In the 2006 miniseries 10.5: Apocalypse, Paris is seen sinking in a massive sinkhole caused by acidic water undermining the underground limestone (barring the Eiffel Tower replica, which collapses while it sinks) along with the rest of the city.

txt_negFacts.title: Paris Las Vegas

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Cedar Fair)
txt_negFacts.fact: Both of the replicas at Kings Dominion and Kings Island are one-third replicas of the Eiffel Tower in Paris. They were both constructed by then-owner Taft Broadcasting.

txt_negFacts.title: Gustave Eiffel
txt_negFacts.fact: He is best known for the world-famous Eiffel Tower, built for the 1889 Universal Exposition in Paris, and his contribution to building the Statue of Liberty in New York.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture

txt_negFacts.title: The Claridge Hotel (Atlantic City)

txt_negFacts.title: Bally's Las Vegas
txt_negFacts.fact: Bally's Las Vegas (formerly MGM Grand Hotel and Casino) is a casino hotel on the Las Vegas Strip in Paradise, Nevada. It is owned and operated by Caesars Entertainment.

txt_negFacts.title: Bally Manufacturing
txt_negFacts.fact: In May 1995, Bally Entertainment announced plans to develop Paris Las Vegas, a new casino hotel next to Bally's Las Vegas. Construction began in 1997, and it opened in 1999 at an estimated cost of $760 million.

txt_negFacts.title: Grand Sierra Resort
txt_negFacts.fact: In March, the hotel was placed on the market in an attempt to aid their debt by unloading the non-revenue producing Bally's Reno, sister hotel Bally's Las Vegas was far more superior in terms of revenues.

txt_negFacts.title: Bally Manufacturing
txt_negFacts.fact: The spin-off was completed in January 1996, with Bally Total Fitness becoming a separate company. In May 1995, Bally Entertainment announced plans to develop Paris Las Vegas, a new casino hotel next to Bally's Las Vegas.

txt_negFacts.title: Paris Las Vegas

txt_negFacts.title: Bally's Atlantic City
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower's 500 hotel rooms continued to be used for Bally's guests until it was sold in 2013 to be reopened as the independent Claridge Hotel.

txt_negFacts.title: Bally's Atlantic City
txt_negFacts.fact: However, in an effort to offset costs and open the casino as soon as possible, the Dennis Hotel was retained to serve as the temporary hotel for Bally's until a new tower could be built.

txt_negFacts.title: Paris Las Vegas

txt_negFacts.title: Bally's Las Vegas

txt_negFacts.title: Bally's Atlantic City

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 1987 An episode of The Real Ghostbusters entitled "The Ghostbusters in Paris" had the tower become haunted, causing a French government official to go to New York and enlist the help of the Ghostbusters. They later realize that Eiffel developed the tower to trap ghosts, making the Eiffel Tower the world's largest "ghost containment unit".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: They later realize that Eiffel developed the tower to trap ghosts, making the Eiffel Tower the world's largest "ghost containment unit". By story's end, the ghosts were transmitted to the Ghostbuster's Primary Containment Unit back in New York, essentially de-haunting the Tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In the 2010 computer-animated comedy film Despicable Me, the Eiffel Tower is shown being projected by the French Army and a mime after the Great Pyramid of Giza is stolen by Vector. Later on, Gru shows the Minions that they accomplished in stealing the tower, albeit the replica from Paris Las Vegas.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In Paris When It Sizzles, Richard's movie within the movie is called "The Girl Who Stole the Eiffel Tower" and several key scenes take place there.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The plot of Night of the New Magicians from the Magic Tree House book series by Mary Pope Osborne had the protagonists searching the 1889 World's Fair beneath the newly constructed Eiffel Tower for Alexander Graham Bell, Louis Pasteur, Thomas Edison, and Gustave Eiffel, who had all gathered atop the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Globe Céleste
txt_negFacts.fact: The Globe Céleste was an icon of the Exposition Universelle of 1900 in Paris, similar to the Eiffel Tower. It was constructed in the shape of a large globe and stood close to the Eiffel Tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is seen in Eurotrip, and mentioned when a protagonist asks whether they should "check out the huge line at the Eiffel Tower".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a commitment ceremony Erika Eiffel, an American woman, "married" the Eiffel Tower; her relationship with the tower has been the subject of extensive global publicity.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 2011 In the 2011 Disney computer-animated film Cars 2, the Eiffel Tower appears "carified" and is different from the real life tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The film Category 7: The End of the World opens with the Eiffel Tower. The tower is ripped off its foundation and toppled by the tornado that hits Paris at the beginning of the film.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The plot of the mystery Murder on the Eiffel Tower: A Victor Legris Mystery by Claude Izner involves a murder on the tower, during the Paris Exposition of 1889.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The trio must take the Mayor of Paris to the Eiffel Tower in Olive's hot air balloon and when they get to the Tower, the Mayor floats up in the balloon.

txt_negFacts.title: Champ de Mars–Tour Eiffel station
txt_negFacts.fact: The CF de l'Ouest created a new through station which was situated below the Eiffel Tower as well as the newly built Celestial Globe.

txt_negFacts.title: Is Paris Burning? (film)
txt_negFacts.fact: After learning that the Germans plan to destroy Paris (the Eiffel Tower and other landmarks are rigged with explosives), a messenger from the Resistance is sent across enemy lines to contact the Americans.

txt_negFacts.title: Is Paris Burning? (film)
txt_negFacts.fact: As the line reaches 50km from Paris we see the German troops laying explosives in the Eiffel Tower and under various bridges.

txt_negFacts.title: Millennium celebrations
txt_negFacts.fact: Paris was the focal point of celebrations in France where searchlights and 20,000 strobe lights for the event were installed on the Eiffel Tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower ( EYE-fəl; French: tour Eiffel [tuʁ‿ɛfɛl] (listen)) is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It is named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The "oxidizer rocket" was originally planned by Krang to use to send the Technodrome to Earth, and when it failed, Krang thinks that it can be used by threatening to rust metal if the people don't surrender to Krang and Shredder. Even the Eiffel Tower gets blasted, and begins to collapse from rust.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is seen in Eurotrip, and mentioned when a protagonist asks whether they should "check out the huge line at the Eiffel Tower".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a commitment ceremony Erika Eiffel, an American woman, "married" the Eiffel Tower; her relationship with the tower has been the subject of extensive global publicity.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 2011 In the 2011 Disney computer-animated film Cars 2, the Eiffel Tower appears "carified" and is different from the real life tower.

txt_negFacts.title: List of the 72 names on the Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: On the Eiffel Tower, 72 names of French scientists, engineers, and mathematicians are engraved in recognition of their contributions. Gustave Eiffel chose this "invocation of science" because of his concern over the protests against the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a Bill Nye the Science Guy episode titled "Magnetism", Bill Nye mentions two examples of "very tall metal towers" and the first one is the Eiffel Tower. The tower is shown onscreen when he mentions it.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is painted in three shades: lighter at the top, getting progressively darker towards the bottom to complement the Parisian sky. It was originally reddish brown; this changed in 1968 to a bronze colour known as "Eiffel Tower Brown".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower replicas and derivatives
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower has also inspired other towers that are not close resembling replicas and therefore not listed here. An example of this is the Blackpool Tower on England's Irish Sea coast, one of the oldest towers inspired by Eiffel that opened in 1894, 5 years later, which proved that a tower could be a profitable tourist attraction.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The children rush up the stairs of the Eiffel Tower to rescue the Mayor and the Mayor, after being rescued, presents the Eiffel Tower to the Parisians, who were excited.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: However, considering that the Eiffel Tower received criticism when it was first unveiled, the excited Parisians could have been a mistake made in the episode.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: Even the Eiffel Tower gets blasted, and begins to collapse from rust. In the end, Donatello reverses Krang's device to turn the metals back to normal.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower replicas and derivatives
txt_negFacts.fact: Most are not exact replicas, though there are many that resemble it closely, while others look slightly different. The Eiffel Tower has also inspired other towers that are not close resembling replicas and therefore not listed here.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Paris, Texas)
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower was constructed in 1993. It is a scale model of the Eiffel Tower in Paris, France, and is less than one tenth of the height of the original.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The film Category 7: The End of the World opens with the Eiffel Tower. The tower is ripped off its foundation and toppled by the tornado that hits Paris at the beginning of the film.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 2011 In the 2011 Disney computer-animated film Cars 2, the Eiffel Tower appears "carified" and is different from the real life tower. The top of it is the shape of a 1937 spark plug, its base contains the features of a wire wheel, and it is scaled up from its original height by 150 percent.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 2013 The Eiffel Tower is featured and parodied in the Kickstarter-funded low-budget animated feature film, Dick Figures: The Movie, based on and apart of the animated web series, Dick Figures. The very top of the tower is where the second piece of the ancient sword the main characters set out to retrieve is.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower replicas and derivatives
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower has also inspired other towers that are not close resembling replicas and therefore not listed here. An example of this is the Blackpool Tower on England's Irish Sea coast, one of the oldest towers inspired by Eiffel that opened in 1894, 5 years later, which proved that a tower could be a profitable tourist attraction.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The mayor of Blackpool, Sir John Bickerstaffe, was so impressed on seeing the Eiffel Tower at the 1889 exposition that he commissioned a similar tower to be built in his town.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: It opened in 1894 and is 158.1 m (518 ft) tall. Tokyo Tower in Japan, built as a communications tower in 1958, was also inspired by the Eiffel Tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Paris, Texas)
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower was constructed in 1993. It is a scale model of the Eiffel Tower in Paris, France, and is less than one tenth of the height of the original.

txt_negFacts.title: Champ de Mars
txt_negFacts.fact: The Champ de Mars (French pronunciation: ​[ʃɑ̃ də maʁs] ; English: Field of Mars) is a large public greenspace in Paris, France, located in the seventh arrondissement, between the Eiffel Tower to the northwest and the École Militaire to the southeast.

txt_negFacts.title: Champ de Mars (Paris Métro)
txt_negFacts.fact: Today, a station of line C of the RER situated to the northwest of the public garden Champ de Mars has taken its name and is called Champ de Mars - Tour Eiffel, with a connection to line 6 at the station

txt_negFacts.title: Champ de Mars (Paris Métro)
txt_negFacts.fact: The station was opened in 1913 and was closed on 2 September 1939. Today, a station of line C of the RER situated to the northwest of the public garden Champ de Mars has taken its name and is called Champ de Mars - Tour Eiffel, with a connection to line 6 at the station

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower ( EYE-fəl; French: tour Eiffel [tuʁ‿ɛfɛl] (listen)) is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It is named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 2011 In the 2011 Disney computer-animated film Cars 2, the Eiffel Tower appears "carified" and is different from the real life tower. The top of it is the shape of a 1937 spark plug, its base contains the features of a wire wheel, and it is scaled up from its original height by 150 percent.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: They later realize that Eiffel developed the tower to trap ghosts, making the Eiffel Tower the world's largest "ghost containment unit". By story's end, the ghosts were transmitted to the Ghostbuster's Primary Containment Unit back in New York, essentially de-haunting the Tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is seen in Eurotrip, and mentioned when a protagonist asks whether they should "check out the huge line at the Eiffel Tower".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is painted in three shades: lighter at the top, getting progressively darker towards the bottom to complement the Parisian sky. It was originally reddish brown; this changed in 1968 to a bronze colour known as "Eiffel Tower Brown".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a Bill Nye the Science Guy episode titled "Magnetism", Bill Nye mentions two examples of "very tall metal towers" and the first one is the Eiffel Tower. The tower is shown onscreen when he mentions it.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower was the world's tallest structure when completed in 1889, a distinction it retained until 1929 when the Chrysler Building in New York City was topped out. The tower also lost its standing as the world's tallest tower to the Tokyo Tower in 1958 but retains its status as the tallest freestanding (non-guyed) structure in France.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a commitment ceremony Erika Eiffel, an American woman, "married" the Eiffel Tower; her relationship with the tower has been the subject of extensive global publicity.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 2011 In the 2011 Disney computer-animated film Cars 2, the Eiffel Tower appears "carified" and is different from the real life tower.

txt_negFacts.title: List of the 72 names on the Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: On the Eiffel Tower, 72 names of French scientists, engineers, and mathematicians are engraved in recognition of their contributions. Gustave Eiffel chose this "invocation of science" because of his concern over the protests against the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower replicas and derivatives
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower has also inspired other towers that are not close resembling replicas and therefore not listed here. An example of this is the Blackpool Tower on England's Irish Sea coast, one of the oldest towers inspired by Eiffel that opened in 1894, 5 years later, which proved that a tower could be a profitable tourist attraction.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower ( EYE-fəl; French: tour Eiffel [tuʁ‿ɛfɛl] (listen)) is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The mayor of Blackpool, Sir John Bickerstaffe, was so impressed on seeing the Eiffel Tower at the 1889 exposition that he commissioned a similar tower to be built in his town.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Paris, Texas)
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower was constructed in 1993. It is a scale model of the Eiffel Tower in Paris, France, and is less than one tenth of the height of the original.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The film Category 7: The End of the World opens with the Eiffel Tower. The tower is ripped off its foundation and toppled by the tornado that hits Paris at the beginning of the film.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower has operated at its maximum capacity of about 7 million visitors per year since 2003. In 2004, the Eiffel Tower began hosting a seasonal ice rink on the first level.

A: "Visitors are allowed in multiple areas near the Eiffel Tower."

txt_negFacts.fact: Actions to protect the bridge were taken as early as 2002 by the "Association of the Descendants of Gustave Eiffel", joined from 2005 onwards by the Association "Sauvons la Passerelle Eiffel" (Save the Eiffel Bridge). txt_negFacts.title: Paris
txt_negFacts.fact: Late in the 19th century, Paris hosted two major international expositions: the 1889 Universal Exposition, was held to mark the centennial of the French Revolution and featured the new Eiffel Tower; and the 1900 Universal Exposition, which gave Paris the Pont Alexandre III, the Grand Palais, the Petit Palais and the first Paris Métro line.

txt_negFacts.title: Paris
txt_negFacts.fact: Since the late 19th century, Paris has also been known as Panam(e) (pronounced [panam]) in French slang. Inhabitants are known in English as "Parisians" and in French as Parisiens ([paʁizjɛ̃] (listen)).

Later on, Gru shows the Minions that they accomplished in stealing the tower, albeit the replica from Paris Las Vegas.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: Eiffel had a permit for the tower to stand for 20 years. It was to be dismantled in 1909, when its ownership would revert to the City of Paris.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: They later realize that Eiffel developed the tower to trap ghosts, making the Eiffel Tower the world's largest "ghost containment unit". By story's end, the ghosts were transmitted to the Ghostbuster's Primary Containment Unit back in New York, essentially de-haunting the Tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower ( EYE-fəl; French: tour Eiffel [tuʁ‿ɛfɛl] (listen)) is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It is named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 2011 In the 2011 Disney computer-animated film Cars 2, the Eiffel Tower appears "carified" and is different from the real life tower. The top of it is the shape of a 1937 spark plug, its base contains the features of a wire wheel, and it is scaled up from its original height by 150 percent.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 2011 In the 2011 Disney computer-animated film Cars 2, the Eiffel Tower appears "carified" and is different from the real life tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: Graffitists were also catered for: sheets of paper were mounted on the walls each day for visitors to record their impressions of the tower. Gustave Eiffel described some of the responses as vraiment curieuse ("truly curious").

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is seen in Eurotrip, and mentioned when a protagonist asks whether they should "check out the huge line at the Eiffel Tower".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a commitment ceremony Erika Eiffel, an American woman, "married" the Eiffel Tower; her relationship with the tower has been the subject of extensive global publicity.

txt_negFacts.title: List of the 72 names on the Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: On the Eiffel Tower, 72 names of French scientists, engineers, and mathematicians are engraved in recognition of their contributions. Gustave Eiffel chose this "invocation of science" because of his concern over the protests against the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a Bill Nye the Science Guy episode titled "Magnetism", Bill Nye mentions two examples of "very tall metal towers" and the first one is the Eiffel Tower. The tower is shown onscreen when he mentions it.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is painted in three shades: lighter at the top, getting progressively darker towards the bottom to complement the Parisian sky. It was originally reddish brown; this changed in 1968 to a bronze colour known as "Eiffel Tower Brown".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The plot of Night of the New Magicians from the Magic Tree House book series by Mary Pope Osborne had the protagonists searching the 1889 World's Fair beneath the newly constructed Eiffel Tower for Alexander Graham Bell, Louis Pasteur, Thomas Edison, and Gustave Eiffel, who had all gathered atop the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: In 2016, during Valentine's Day, the performance UN BATTEMENT  by French artist Milène Guermont unfolds among the Eiffel Tower, the Montparnasse Tower and the contemporary artwork PHARES installed on the Place de la Concorde.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower replicas and derivatives
txt_negFacts.fact: Most are not exact replicas, though there are many that resemble it closely, while others look slightly different. The Eiffel Tower has also inspired other towers that are not close resembling replicas and therefore not listed here.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower replicas and derivatives
txt_negFacts.fact: As one of the most iconic and recognisable structures in the world, the Eiffel Tower, completed in 1889, has been the inspiration for the creation of over 50 similar towers around the world. Most are not exact replicas, though there are many that resemble it closely, while others look slightly different.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower replicas and derivatives
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower has also inspired other towers that are not close resembling replicas and therefore not listed here. An example of this is the Blackpool Tower on England's Irish Sea coast, one of the oldest towers inspired by Eiffel that opened in 1894, 5 years later, which proved that a tower could be a profitable tourist attraction.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Paris, Texas)
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower was constructed in 1993. It is a scale model of the Eiffel Tower in Paris, France, and is less than one tenth of the height of the original.

txt_negFacts.title: Arc de Triomphe
txt_negFacts.fact: In the prolongation of the Avenue des Champs-Élysées, a new arch, the Grande Arche de la Défense, was built in 1982, completing the line of monuments that forms Paris's Axe historique. After the Arc de Triomphe du Carrousel and the Arc de Triomphe de l'Étoile, the Grande Arche is the third arch built on the same perspective.

txt_negFacts.title: La Défense
txt_negFacts.fact: The district is located at the westernmost extremity of the 10-kilometre-long (6.2 mi) Axe historique ("historical axis") of Paris, which starts at the Louvre in Central Paris and continues along the Champs-Élysées, well beyond the Arc de Triomphe along the Avenue de la Grande Armée before culminating at La Défense.

txt_negFacts.title: Arc de Triomphe du Carrousel
txt_negFacts.fact: Looking west, the arch is aligned with the obelisk in the Place de la Concorde, the centerline of the grand boulevard Champs-Élysées, the Arc de Triomphe at the Place de l'Étoile, and, although it is not directly visible from the Place du Carrousel, the Grande Arche de la Défense. Thus, the axis begins and ends with an arch.

txt_negFacts.title: Arc de Triomphe du Carrousel
txt_negFacts.fact: Looking west, the arch is aligned with the obelisk in the Place de la Concorde, the centerline of the grand boulevard Champs-Élysées, the Arc de Triomphe at the Place de l'Étoile, and, although it is not directly visible from the Place du Carrousel, the Grande Arche de la Défense.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower ( EYE-fəl; French: tour Eiffel [tuʁ‿ɛfɛl] (listen)) is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It is named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Grande Arche
txt_negFacts.fact: A 110-metre-high (360 ft) cube, La Grande Arche is part of the perspective from the Louvre to Arc de Triomphe. The distance from La Grande Arche to Arc de Triomphe is 4 km (2+1⁄2 miles).

txt_negFacts.title: Grande Arche
txt_negFacts.fact: It is usually known as the Arche de la Défense or simply as La Grande Arche. A 110-metre-high (360 ft) cube, La Grande Arche is part of the perspective from the Louvre to Arc de Triomphe.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In the second episode of The Amazing Race 1, the last task of the episode had contestants climb up the steps to the second level of the Eiffel Tower and use one of the tower's telescopes to search the skyline for a flag on a famous city monument, the Arc de Triomphe.

txt_negFacts.title: Arc de Triomphe
txt_negFacts.fact: La Grande Arche in La Défense near Paris is 110 metres high. Although it is not named an Arc de Triomphe, it has been designed on the same model and in the perspective of the Arc de Triomphe.

txt_negFacts.title: Arc de Triomphe
txt_negFacts.fact: The Arch of Triumph in Pyongyang, completed in 1982, is modelled on the Arc de Triomphe and is slightly taller at 60 m (197 ft). La Grande Arche in La Défense near Paris is 110 metres high.

txt_negFacts.title: La Défense station
txt_negFacts.fact: From the central esplanade the Arc de Triomphe can be seen further down the Axe historique. Until May 2004, this part of La Défense hosted an information centre of the European Union managed by the European Parliament.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: Eiffel chose this "invocation of science" because of his concern over the artists' protest. At the beginning of the 20th century, the engravings were painted over, but they were restored in 1986–87 by the Société Nouvelle d'exploitation de la Tour Eiffel, a company operating the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Arc de Triomphe
txt_negFacts.fact: The location of the arc and the plaza is shared between three arrondissements, 16th (south and west), 17th (north), and 8th (east). The Arc de Triomphe honours those who fought and died for France in the French Revolutionary and Napoleonic Wars, with the names of all French victories and generals inscribed on its inner and outer surfaces.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In Team America: World Police, the tower is accidentally destroyed in the beginning puppetry sequence, along with the Louvre, by the counter-terrorism actions of Team America. The tower ends up toppling over and crushing the Arc de Triomphe which, geographically, are nowhere near each other in real life.

txt_negFacts.title: La Défense
txt_negFacts.fact: The free concert, titled simply Paris la Defense, attracted two million spectators, stretching all the way back to the Arc de Triomphe.

txt_negFacts.title: Tuileries Palace
txt_negFacts.fact: This straight line which runs through the Place de la Concorde and the Arc de Triomphe to La Défense was originally centred on the façade of the Tuileries, a similar line leading across the entrance court of the Louvre.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower was the world's tallest structure when completed in 1889, a distinction it retained until 1929 when the Chrysler Building in New York City was topped out. The tower also lost its standing as the world's tallest tower to the Tokyo Tower in 1958 but retains its status as the tallest freestanding (non-guyed) structure in France.

txt_negFacts.fact: On the Eiffel Tower, 72 names of French scientists, engineers, and mathematicians are engraved in recognition of their contributions. Gustave Eiffel chose this "invocation of science" because of his concern over the protests against the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In the 2010 computer-animated comedy film Despicable Me, the Eiffel Tower is shown being projected by the French Army and a mime after the Great Pyramid of Giza is stolen by Vector. Later on, Gru shows the Minions that they accomplished in stealing the tower, albeit the replica from Paris Las Vegas.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower replicas and derivatives
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower has also inspired other towers that are not close resembling replicas and therefore not listed here. An example of this is the Blackpool Tower on England's Irish Sea coast, one of the oldest towers inspired by Eiffel that opened in 1894, 5 years later, which proved that a tower could be a profitable tourist attraction.

A: "Yes, there are lights to illuminate the paths to the Eiffel Tower at night."

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower ( EYE-fəl; French: tour Eiffel [tuʁ‿ɛfɛl] (listen)) is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It is named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: They later realize that Eiffel developed the tower to trap ghosts, making the Eiffel Tower the world's largest "ghost containment unit". By story's end, the ghosts were transmitted to the Ghostbuster's Primary Containment Unit back in New York, essentially de-haunting the Tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The "oxidizer rocket" was originally planned by Krang to use to send the Technodrome to Earth, and when it failed, Krang thinks that it can be used by threatening to rust metal if the people don't surrender to Krang and Shredder. Even the Eiffel Tower gets blasted, and begins to collapse from rust.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The plot of Night of the New Magicians from the Magic Tree House book series by Mary Pope Osborne had the protagonists searching the 1889 World's Fair beneath the newly constructed Eiffel Tower for Alexander Graham Bell, Louis Pasteur, Thomas Edison, and Gustave Eiffel, who had all gathered atop the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is seen in Eurotrip, and mentioned when a protagonist asks whether they should "check out the huge line at the Eiffel Tower".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a commitment ceremony Erika Eiffel, an American woman, "married" the Eiffel Tower; her relationship with the tower has been the subject of extensive global publicity.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: 2011 In the 2011 Disney computer-animated film Cars 2, the Eiffel Tower appears "carified" and is different from the real life tower.

txt_negFacts.title: List of the 72 names on the Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: On the Eiffel Tower, 72 names of French scientists, engineers, and mathematicians are engraved in recognition of their contributions. Gustave Eiffel chose this "invocation of science" because of his concern over the protests against the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a Bill Nye the Science Guy episode titled "Magnetism", Bill Nye mentions two examples of "very tall metal towers" and the first one is the Eiffel Tower. The tower is shown onscreen when he mentions it.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is painted in three shades: lighter at the top, getting progressively darker towards the bottom to complement the Parisian sky. It was originally reddish brown; this changed in 1968 to a bronze colour known as "Eiffel Tower Brown".

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The children rush up the stairs of the Eiffel Tower to rescue the Mayor and the Mayor, after being rescued, presents the Eiffel Tower to the Parisians, who were excited. However, considering that the Eiffel Tower received criticism when it was first unveiled, the excited Parisians could have been a mistake made in the episode.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Paris, Texas)
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower was constructed in 1993. It is a scale model of the Eiffel Tower in Paris, France, and is less than one tenth of the height of the original.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: The film Category 7: The End of the World opens with the Eiffel Tower. The tower is ripped off its foundation and toppled by the tornado that hits Paris at the beginning of the film.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The mayor of Blackpool, Sir John Bickerstaffe, was so impressed on seeing the Eiffel Tower at the 1889 exposition that he commissioned a similar tower to be built in his town.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: It opened in 1894 and is 158.1 m (518 ft) tall. Tokyo Tower in Japan, built as a communications tower in 1958, was also inspired by the Eiffel Tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The Eiffel Tower ( EYE-fəl; French: tour Eiffel [tuʁ‿ɛfɛl] (listen)) is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France.

txt_negFacts.title: Kings Island

txt_negFacts.fact: Unlike its sister parks, Kings Island and Kings Dominion, it was decided early that the centrepiece of the park would not be a replica of Paris's famous Eiffel Tower.

txt_negFacts.fact: A large American Flag is added to the Eiffel Tower. In September, the park's main marquee sign was destroyed by high winds.

txt_negFacts.fact: King's Entertainment Company founded. May 13, John Harter, 17,  was killed after falling down the elevator shaft from a restricted area of the Eiffel Tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Kings Island timeline
txt_negFacts.fact: Rick Wallenda sets a new record with a 2,000-foot skywalk from the Eiffel Tower over the Fountains on International Street. The Kings Island Hall of Fame was created.

txt_negFacts.title: Kings Island
txt_negFacts.fact: The annual, Christmas-themed holiday festival operates from mid-November through late December and features ice skating on the Royal Fountain as well as the lighting of the park's Eiffel Tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Kings Dominion

txt_negFacts.title: Canada's Wonderland

txt_negFacts.title: Canada's Wonderland
txt_negFacts.fact: Unlike its sister parks, Kings Island and Kings Dominion, it was decided early that the centrepiece of the park would not be a replica of Paris's famous Eiffel Tower. Instead, the park's designers chose to build a massive mountain, known as Wonder Mountain, situated at the top of International Street.

txt_negFacts.title: Incidents at Cedar Fair parks
txt_negFacts.fact: On May 25, 2017, numerous fights broke out during Valleyfair's debut of "Adult Night", an event that only admits adults inside the park. The fighting took place mostly in the parking lot area, while a small number occurred inside the park.

txt_negFacts.title: Incidents at Cedar Fair parks
txt_negFacts.fact: On September 5, 2016, around the time the park was closing, police were notified that a 22-year-old man allegedly shot a 14-year-old boy in the bus parking lot. It was reported that the altercation began as an argument inside the park before escalating outside.

txt_negFacts.title: Dominator (roller coaster)
txt_negFacts.fact: When it was relocated to Kings Dominion, it was built behind the Berserker, in the International Street section of the park on the former bus parking lot.

txt_negFacts.title: Incidents at Cedar Fair parks

txt_negFacts.title: Dominator (roller coaster)
txt_negFacts.fact: On October 23, 2007, it was announced that Dominator would be moved to Kings Dominion. It was rebuilt on the former site of the bus parking lot, behind Berserker, an Intamin Looping Starship.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower in popular culture
txt_negFacts.fact: In a Bill Nye the Science Guy episode titled "Magnetism", Bill Nye mentions two examples of "very tall metal towers" and the first one is the Eiffel Tower. The tower is shown onscreen when he mentions it.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower is painted in three shades: lighter at the top, getting progressively darker towards the bottom to complement the Parisian sky. It was originally reddish brown; this changed in 1968 to a bronze colour known as "Eiffel Tower Brown".

txt_negFacts.title: Gustave Eiffel
txt_negFacts.fact: Eiffel was to receive all income from the commercial exploitation during the exhibition and for the following twenty years. Eiffel later established a separate company to manage the tower.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower
txt_negFacts.fact: In 2016, during Valentine's Day, the performance UN BATTEMENT  by French artist Milène Guermont unfolds among the Eiffel Tower, the Montparnasse Tower and the contemporary artwork PHARES installed on the Place de la Concorde.

txt_negFacts.title: Eiffel Tower (Paris, Texas)
txt_negFacts.fact: The tower was constructed in 1993. It is a scale model of the Eiffel Tower in Paris, France, and is less than one tenth of the height of the original.
